channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Canada Invests $400M in British Columbia to Boost Critical Minerals Supply Chains
item.description: The Canadian government has agreed to contribute up to C$400 million to Teck Resources Ltd.'s plans to expand output at its Trail zinc-and-lead smelter in British Columbia. This investment, part of th...

item.title: Data Centers Drive Up Power Costs for Rust Belt Manufacturers
item.description: The article discusses how the proliferation of power-hungry data centers serving the artificial intelligence industry is causing significant increases in electricity bills for manufacturers in the Rus...

item.title: Netflix expands into short-form video with major media partnerships
item.description: Netflix is intensifying its push into the short-form video market by signing licensing agreements with prominent U.S. publishers like Penske Media, BuzzFeed Studios, Conde Nast, Hearst Magazines, and ...

item.title: Government Plans New Multi-Sector Measures to Strengthen Wetland Protection Across Tanzania
item.description: The Tanzanian government has announced a new approach to address the ongoing problem of wetland encroachment after acknowledging that the existing legal framework has not been enough to protect these ...

item.title: National Treasury withholds funds from municipalities to enforce fiscal discipline
item.description: National Treasury has temporarily withheld R24.12bn in equitable share transfers to 69 municipalities to address persistent fruitless and wasteful expenditure (UIFWE). This measure aims to enforce fis...

item.title: Toronto Blue Jays defeat San Francisco Giants 9-3 in season opener
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays secured a decisive 9-3 victory over the San Francisco Giants in a matchup that showcased strong offensive performances. Jonatan Clase, recalled from the minors on July 1, made an...

item.title: Mbwana Ally Samatta Announces Retirement from Tanzania National Team After 15-Year Career
item.description: Mbwana Ally Samatta, captain of Tanzania's national football team and a celebrated athlete, has officially retired from international football after 15 years of service to the Taifa Stars. The 33-year...

item.title: Keumbu Market Paralyzed by Social Media Boycott After Political Violence
item.description: Small-scale traders at Keumbu Market are facing severe losses after a social media boycott campaign targeted the roadside market following an attack on the Linda Mwananchi brigade. The incident occurr...

item.title: Melbourne's Havelock Place: A Fusion of Sri Lankan and Australian Flavors
item.description: The Havelock Place, located in Melbourne's Canterbury precinct, has been transformed into a unique dining destination that blends Sri Lankan and Australian culinary traditions. Originally a historic p...

item.title: Switzerland advances to FIFA World Cup quarter-finals after penalty shootout victory over Colombia
item.description: Switzerland secured their place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-finals after a dramatic 4-3 penalty shootout victory over Colombia in Vancouver, Canada. The match, which marked the final game outsi...

item.title: High Court dismisses Prince Harry and other claimants' phone hacking case against Associated Newspapers
item.description: The High Court has dismissed in full the phone hacking and unlawful information gathering claims brought by Prince Harry, Baroness Doreen Lawrence and five other high-profile claimants against Associa...

item.title: BJP Likely to Win Bengal Rajya Sabha Seats Amid TMC Faction Withdrawal
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is poised to secure all three Rajya Sabha seats from West Bengal in upcoming bypolls, as both factions of the Trinamool Congress (TMC) have opted not to contest. This ...

item.title: MRT-7 roof installation causes temporary lane closures on North Avenue
item.description: The Department of Transportation (DOTr) announced the temporary closure of two southbound lanes of North Avenue from Sugar Center to Ninoy Aquino Park and Wildlife Center starting July 8, 2026, to fac...

item.title: El-Rufai’s Wife Appeals to Tinubu for Fair Treatment, Bail Rights and Access to Medical Care
item.description: Hajiya Asiya El-Rufai, wife of former Kaduna State Governor Nasir El-Rufai, has appealed to President Bola Ahmed Tinubu to ensure that her husband receives fair treatment while he remains in detention...

item.title: Feasibility of Housing a T-Rex in a Nature Reserve: Challenges and Considerations
item.description: The article explores the hypothetical scenario of whether a Tyrannosaurus rex could realistically be kept in a nature reserve today. It highlights the immense size of a T-Rex, which could grow up to 4...

item.title: Karachi's ranking as one of the least liveable cities in 2026
item.description: Karachi has been ranked 170th out of 173 cities in the Economist Intelligence Unit’s Global Liveability Index 2026, placing it among the least liveable cities globally. The ranking highlights challeng...

item.title: US-Iran tensions escalate after strikes, India-Pakistan relations under spotlight, and global news updates
item.description: The article highlights major international developments on July 8, 2026. US forces launched airstrikes on Iran following attacks on commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, escalating regional tens...

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Quarter-Final Line-Up Revealed: Top Teams Battle in US
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 has narrowed down to eight teams, with the quarter-final matches set to take place across the United States. The draw features high-profile clashes between France and Morocco, ...

item.title: First Nation calls for consultation on proposed Alberta-Sarnia oil pipeline amid environmental concerns
item.description: The chief of Aamjiwnaang First Nation, Janelle Nahmabin, has criticized the lack of consultation regarding a proposed oil pipeline between Alberta and Sarnia, Ontario. The project, announced by Albert...

item.title: A Comparative Review of Parmesan Cheeses from Australian Supermarkets
item.description: The article details an in-depth taste test of various parmesan cheeses available in Australian supermarkets. It evaluates brands such as Coles, Aldi, and specialty stores, highlighting their unique ch...
